text:
In a small, quiet town nestled between rolling hills, there was a young girl named Lucy who lived in her own little world filled with fantasy. Every evening, after finishing her homework, Lucy would close her book on tiny fairies and head outdoors, finding joy in the rustling leaves and the songbirds singing.

One day, her favorite tree, an old oak known far and wide for its beautiful, swirling spiral bark, dropped an acorn into Lucy's shoe while she was playing. She shook it off onto the driveway, intrigued. The next morning, her mom saw the acorn there, staring back at her as if it had eyes.

"Weeping willow tree?" her mom guessed.

"Yes! That's my special tree!" Lucy replied with excitement. She collected acorns all winter long, dreaming of a giant oak tree filled with her own tiny birdies. When spring came, she climbed up into the branches, finding it warm and comfortable.

Each day she would visit her imaginary friend
